event: ICC Men's T20 World Cup — Final
video_reference_note: Official match highlights (ICC): india-script-stunning-title-win-match-highlights-sa-v-ind-t20wc-2024-final — plus extended highlights on ICC.tv. This JSON does not host video.
disclaimer: PROTOTYPE: Transcript simulates ASR output from a highlight reel (video → audio → speech-to-text). Not an official broadcast script. Academic/portfolio use only. Aligns with dataset match_id 1415755 (India vs South Africa, T20 WC 2024 Final).
highlight_transcript:
Kensington Oval is packed for the biggest game of the year — India against South Africa in the T20 World Cup final. Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli walk out under lights. Marco Jansen runs in first change of ends — full and shaping in, Rohit knocks it quietly for a single, sensibly keeping the strike rotating. Next over Kohli comes alive — pitched up outside off and he creams the drive through point, sublime timing for four. Then he whips one off the pads with his trademark flick — boundary again, the crowd erupts. This is dominant batting from a master on the grandest stage.

India build through the middle but South Africa drag it back — dot balls mount, batters defended and blocked as the squeeze tightens. One batter mistimes a loft over cover — it lands safe inside the rope for two, not quite the six they wanted. Another pushes early at a cutter and is beaten on the inside edge onto pad — huge appeal, tension everywhere.

India finish with a fighting total on the board. South Africa's chase starts with drama — an outswinger squares the batter up, beaten on the drive, edge in the air but it falls short. Then a short ball is pulled — mistimed, top edge, hearts in mouths before it lands in space. The partnership grows — crisp drives through cover for four, a maximum over long on when the slot ball arrives. Quinton de Kock works singles, classically placed into the gaps.

India turn to spin and hard lengths — wickets in clusters, the required rate climbs. David Miller stands tall — hammered down the ground, bludgeoned when it's full, South Africa still believe. But the death overs are chaos — yorkers, slower balls, batters beaten outside off as they swing for the ropes. A skyer goes up — a stunning catch at the boundary, athleticism under pressure, the stadium roars.

Sixteen needed off the final few deliveries — every ball defended or scrambled feels like a win for the fielding side. A mistimed heave, a dot, a scramble — the squeeze is unbearable. In the end India hold their nerve by a slender margin — seven runs — and the drought is over. The highlight reel captured every swing of momentum: beaten edges, dominant boundaries, defensive blocks in the middle, and a finish for the ages.
